title: Establish statistical significance of a delta
description: Decide whether a baseline-vs-candidate difference is real by comparing distributions, not single means, accounting for variance.
body: Steps: 1. Collect multiple independent samples for baseline and candidate after warmup. 2. Summarize each with median and a spread measure (IQR/stddev), not just the mean. 3. Compare distributions (e.g. confidence interval on the difference or a nonparametric test) rather than two point values. 4. Reject deltas smaller than the measured noise floor. 5. Record sample count, summary stats, and the decision. Rollback point: before declaring a regression or improvement. Validation: the delta exceeds the noise band with a stated confidence and repeats across runs; a single faster run inside the variance is not evidence of improvement.
